{{Quote|The official designation is the Mountain Terrain Scout Transport, but they look like giant, six-legged bugs.|Imperial Cadet Myrette Davani|<ref name="Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 3" />}}
The '''Mountain Terrain Scout Transport''' ('''MT-ST'''), also known as the "'''bug walker'''" for its six-[[Limb/Legends|legged]], [[Insect/Legends|insect]]-like appearance, was a ground [[Walker/Legends|walker]] in the service of the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] during the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Measuring 7.2 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height and comparable to a large [[Landspeeder/Legends|speeder]] in length, the MT-ST moved at a rapid gait of 100 [[Kilometer per hour/Legends|km/h]]. The walker was armed with a single twin [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannon]] and afforded room for only a single [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] to operate.

[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Cadet/Legends|cadets]] at the [[Carida Academy/Legends|Academy of Carida]] first trained with [[Flight simulator/Legends|simulated]] and later prototype versions of the MT-ST in the [[Standard month/Legends|months]] following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. In one such war-games exercise around [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], Cadet [[Lumiya|Shira Brie]], secretly a pupil of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], used a prototype MT-ST that was intended to be equipped with training [[Laser/Legends|lasers]] but was actually fully armed to crush a competing [[Bantha Squad|squad]], [[Death/Legends|killing]] six fellow cadets.

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|I have to admit the things looked a little creepy from the outside after spending so much time in the simulated cockpit. […] They're about as long as a good-size speeder, with room for one pilot and a tiny cargo hold that made me think these things would never be landed without heavier support.|Myrette Davani|<ref name="Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 3" />}}
The Mountain Terrain Scout Transport (MT-ST) was a model of ground [[Walker/Legends|walker]] utilized by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Colloquially termed the "[[Bug (creature)|bug]] walker" for its [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] appearance, the MT-ST stood on six [[Limb/Legends|legs]] and, according to Imperial [[Cadet/Legends|Cadet]] [[Myrette Davani]], looked "a little creepy" upon external inspection. The MT-ST stood 7.2 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height and measured about the length of a large [[Landspeeder/Legends|speeder]]. The walker's interior held room for a single [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and no passengers, and included a small cargo hold with a carrying capacity of thirty [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]]. Armed with a single fire-linked twin [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannon]] with a firing range of 200 meters, the MT-ST achieved a maximum velocity of 100 [[Kilometer per hour/Legends|kilometers per hour]] and, by Davani's judgment, moved quickly even on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Carida/Legends|Carida's]] high-[[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] surface.<ref name="Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 3" />

In combat situations, the MT-ST would normally be deployed via [[Drop pod/Legends|drop pod]]. Cadets in training could have the MT-ST prototype up and running in less than five [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]]. The MT-ST was not available for public sale.<ref name="Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 3" />

==History==
{{Quote|By the time I got there (in under two minutes, I'm happy to say) Shira had single-handedly decimated Bantha squad. Normally, this would have meant that the training lasers in her MT-ST had scored hits on her targets, and automated sensors would have shut the target vehicles down. But Shira's bug walker hadn't been equipped with training lasers. Her vehicle had been fully armed. She'd killed half a dozen of our fellow students and totaled twice as many MT-STs when I found her […]|Myrette Davani|<ref name="Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 3" />}}
The MT-ST walker entered the arsenal of the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]]<ref name="Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 2">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030911carida2|series=Carida: Heavy Duty|text=Part 2: Boot Camp}}</ref> in the [[Standard month/Legends|months]] following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 1">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030904carida1|series=Carida: Heavy Duty|text=Part 1: Weight Training}}</ref> By [[0 ABY/Legends|eight months]] after [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]],<ref name="Date1">[[Carida: Heavy Duty|''Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 1: Weight Training'']] establishes that [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Cadet/Legends|Cadets]] [[Lumiya|Shira Brie]] and [[Myrette Davani]] begin their [[Standard year/Legends|year]] of study at the [[Carida Academy/Legends|Academy of Carida]] six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. [[Carida: Heavy Duty|''Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 2: Boot Camp'']] is set two months into their year of study, or eight months after the Battle of [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]].</ref> Cadets Myrette Davani and [[Lumiya|Shira Brie]] at the Imperial [[Carida Academy/Legends|Academy of Carida]] trained in [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] [[Flight simulator/Legends|simulations]] of the new walker in preparation for eventual use of the MT-ST in an upcoming war-games competition.<ref name="Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 2" /> In approximately [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="Date2">[[Carida: Heavy Duty|''Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 3: War Games'']] takes place midway through [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Cadet/Legends|Cadets]] [[Lumiya|Shira Brie]] and [[Myrette Davani]]'s [[Standard year/Legends|year]] of study at the [[Carida Academy/Legends|Academy of Carida]], which [[Carida: Heavy Duty|''Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 1: Weight Training'']] establishes to begin six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Therefore, Part 3 is set approximately a year after the Battle of [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]], or [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]].</ref> cadets at the Academy finally tested MT-ST prototypes in the field, going head-to-head in the jagged and [[Mountain/Legends|mountainous]] Caridan war-games range known as "[[Tarkin's Teeth]]."<ref name="Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 3" />

The competing cadets and the MT-ST prototypes were both delivered to the range aboard [[Dropship/Legends|drop ships]]. Davani, Brie, and a host of supporting cadets formed one team, while another group, named [[Bantha Squad]], comprised their opposition. The prototype walkers that the cadets piloted for the war-games exercise were outfitted with training [[Laser/Legends|lasers]] and were designed to shut down when automated [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] registered that the vehicles were hit—all except for Brie's MT-ST, which, unknown to the rest of the cadets, was instead fully armed. Davani and Brie set out alone in search of Bantha Squad, leaving the rest of their team behind to guard their assigned perimeter, and Brie quickly found her targets first. Within minutes, Brie single-handedly wiped out the other team, [[Death/Legends|killing]] six of her fellow cadets and totaling a dozen MT-STs in the process.<ref name="Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 3" />

Much to Davani's consternation, no one in authority at the Academy took any action against Brie, whom<ref name="Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 3" /> the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]<ref name="CSWE-Darth Vader">{{CSWECite|III|288–290|Vader, Darth}}</ref> [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] had personally selected for accelerated training in [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]].<ref name="CSWE-Lumiya">{{CSWECite|II|265|Lumiya}}</ref> The Academy wrote off her MT-ST's charged lasers to "instructor error," and the deaths of the cadets as "regrettable casualties." Davani later recorded the incident and her experience training with the MT-ST in a series of [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] messages to [[Nils Davani|her]] [[Haryette Davani|parents]],<ref name="Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 3" /> which were eventually obtained by [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Rebel Alliance Intelligence]].<ref name="Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 2" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Mountain Terrain Scout Transport was created by former ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' editor [[Cory J. Herndon]]<ref name="Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 2" /> for the [[September]] [[2003]] [[Wizards.com]] ''[[Planet Hoppers]]'' article series entry entitled ''[[Carida: Heavy Duty]]'', which focused on Shira Brie's training career at the Academy of Carida.<ref name="Carida: Heavy Duty">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030904carida|text=Carida: Heavy Duty|int=Carida: Heavy Duty}}</ref> Herndon first referenced the MT-ST as a new vehicle with which the Academy cadets were training in the second entry of the Carida article series, [[Carida: Heavy Duty|''Boot Camp'']], released on [[September 11]].<ref name="Carida: Heavy Duty, Part 2" />

The MT-ST shares a basic similarity with the [[Mountain Terrain Armored Transport]], a walker design introduced in the [[1994]] [[Star Wars: The Jedi Academy Trilogy|''The Jedi Academy Trilogy'']] novel series by author [[Kevin J. Anderson]].<ref name="Dark Apprentice">[[Dark Apprentice (novel)|''Dark Apprentice'']]</ref><ref name="Champions of the Force">''[[Champions of the Force]]''</ref> However, no specific relationship exists between the MT-ST and MT-AT.
